# Test-Quality Review: daily_os_next/agents/state

**Scope:** `lib/features/daily_os_next/agents/state/` (1 impl file + 1 generated file) and
`test/features/daily_os_next/agents/state/` (1 test file).

Generated by automated test-quality review (static analysis).

---

## File inventory

| File | Lines | Has test? | Top issue |
|------|-------|-----------|-----------|
| `lib/.../state/day_agent_providers.dart` | 127 | Yes | `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` has no dedicated wiring test |
| `lib/.../state/day_agent_providers.g.dart` | 539 | — | Generated; not in scope |
| `test/.../state/day_agent_providers_test.dart` | 353 | — | Per-test inline `ProviderContainer` setup repeated 8 times; no shared helper |

---

## File size / split opportunities

- [ ] **[LOW]** `day_agent_providers.dart` (127 lines) and `day_agent_providers_test.dart` (353 lines) are both well within acceptable bounds. No file split is needed.

---

## Test quality improvements

- [ ] **[HIGH]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart` — each of the 8 test bodies independently constructs `MockXxx` dependencies, `ProviderContainer`, and `addTearDown`. This is ~35 repeated local variable declarations across ~330 lines. Extract a file-level `_buildContainer({required ...overrides})` helper that accepts only the varying overrides as named parameters. The pattern is identical to what `AGENTS.md` calls out explicitly ("Extract pump/setup helpers"). This is the single biggest quality gap in the state test file.
- [ ] **[MED]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart:21-61` — `dayAgentServiceProvider` wiring test asserts all six dependency identities via `same(...)` and verifies the notification callback. The same mock + container + callback pattern is copy-pasted verbatim in the `dayAgentPlanServiceProvider` test at lines 63–97. These two tests are similar enough that a shared `_buildServiceContainer` helper with a `dependencies` map would eliminate the duplication.
- [ ] **[MED]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart` — `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` (lines 32–45 of the impl) is never tested in its own `group`. It is only used as an override in other provider tests. The wiring test should verify that `DayAgentCaptureService` receives `agentRepository`, `syncService`, `journalDb`, `journalRepository`, `fts5Db`, `orchestrator`, `domainLogger`, and the notifications callback — consistent with how `dayAgentServiceProvider` and `dayAgentPlanServiceProvider` are verified. This is a genuine missing-behavior gap.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `day_agent_providers_test.dart:99-127` — `dayAgentProvider` test uses `makeTestIdentity` and asserts `result == identity`, then verifies `getDayAgentForDate` was called exactly once. The test is meaningful and DRY given its single scenario, but a "returns null when service returns null" negative path is absent.

---

## Generative (Glados) testing opportunities

- [ ] **[LOW]** The providers in this file are all Riverpod wiring glue (dependency injection + async delegation). There is no pure domain logic, state machine, or data transformation to exercise generatively. Glados is not a fit here.

---

## Coverage / missing-behavior gaps

- [x] **[HIGH]** `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` — the provider is defined at `lib/.../state/day_agent_providers.dart:32-45` and wires `agentRepository`, `syncService`, `journalDb`, `journalRepository`, `fts5Db`, `orchestrator`, `domainLogger`, and notifications. None of these wiring assignments are verified by a dedicated test group. Only its service object is used as an override in other tests. **RESOLVED:** new 'dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider wires dependencies and persisted-state notifications' test — overrides all seven Riverpod dependencies (Fts5Db swapped in via `setUpTestGetIt`), asserts each wired field is `same(...)` as its override, and verifies `onPersistedStateChanged` forwards through `notifyUiOnly` with the agent notification.
- [ ] **[MED]** `dayAgentProvider` negative path — `getDayAgentForDate` returning `null` is not tested. The provider propagates `null` directly; a test would catch any inadvertent null-guard change.
- [ ] **[LOW]** `parsedItemsForCaptureProvider` — the "capture not found" branch (`getCapture` returns `null`, so `agentUpdateStreamProvider` is never watched) is not exercised. The provider silently falls through to `parsedItemsForCapture` even when no capture exists; this path should be explicitly tested.

---

## Test execution speed opportunities

- [ ] **[LOW]** All tests are synchronous Riverpod container reads (`.future` awaited directly); no real I/O, no timers, no `Future.delayed`. No fake-time violations detected.
- [ ] **[LOW]** The repeated mock object creation in each test body (8 times × 3–8 `MockXxx()` instantiations) adds minor per-test overhead, but the mocks themselves are trivial to construct. Extracting the shared helper (see Test Quality section) would incidentally eliminate this overhead.

---

## Summary

- 0 oversized impl files; 0 oversized test files.
- 1 genuine missing-behavior gap: `dayAgentCaptureServiceProvider` has no wiring test (`[HIGH]`).
- 1 major DRY violation: 8 tests each build their own `ProviderContainer` inline; a shared helper is required (`[HIGH]`).
- 2 negative-path gaps: `dayAgentProvider`/`parsedItemsForCaptureProvider` null-return branches untested (`[MED]`/`[LOW]`).
- No Glados opportunities (pure wiring code), no fake-time violations, all mocks come from `test/mocks/mocks.dart`.
- Biggest opportunity: extract a `_buildContainer` factory to collapse ~300 lines of boilerplate into reusable setup.
